summarization.a: The figure displays X-ray diffraction patterns for 500nm Ba0.6Sr0.4TiO3 (BST) thin films deposited on Si substrates with different buffer layers, i) Buffer layer of 50nm TiO2 ii) Buffer layer of 50nm Ta2O5

data_extraction.a:
| 2θ (degree) | Intensity (arb. units) a BST/TiO2/Si | Intensity (arb. units) a BST/Ta2O5/Si |
|---|---|---|
| 22 | BST (100) | BST (100) |
|27 | TiO₂ (101) |  -                 |
| 33 | BST (110) | BST (110) |
| 38 |  -                 | Ta₂O₅ (200) |
| 40 | BST (111) |  BST (111) |
| 45 | BST (200) | BST (200) | 
| 52 | BST (210) | BST (210) |
| 56 | BST (211) | BST (211) |

a.question: Which is the highly preferred orientation of BST?
a.answer: BST (110) is present and it has the highest intensity in both cases BST/TiO2/Si and BST/Ta2O5/Si

a.question: List the intense BST peaks for both BST/TiO2/Si and BST/Ta2O5/Si
a.answer:
For BST/Ta2O5/Si, the following BST peaks are observed
(100)
(110)
(111)
(200)
(211)

 and for  BST/TiO2/Si
(100)
(110)
(200)
(211)

a.question: Do the BST films show single phase crystallinity?
How evident are the peaks of buffer layers?
a.answer: For Ta2O5, the (200) oriented peaks at 27 and 38 degrees can be seen as tiny bumps and TiO2 the (101) orientation is seen around 25 degree. These peaks are very small in intensity compared to the rest of the BST peaks.
